Title: Metabolomics navigates natural variation in pathogen-induced secondary metabolism across soybean cultivar populations
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: dirigent family protein; putative coumestrol biosynthesis regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased coumestrol accumulation and enhanced resistance to Phytophthora sojae in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dir2a was shortlisted from tissue metabolite–transcript correlation and pathogen-inducible transcript analyses as a coumestrol-biosynthesis candidate. 2. Transient overexpression in Williams 82 hairy roots significantly elevated coumestrol content. 3. Overexpression in susceptible cultivar P20 increased coumestrol and reduced P. sojae infection biomass by about 70%.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Coumestrol biosynthesis module; exact direct downstream enzymatic targets not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator/candidate determinant enhancing coumestrol accumulation in roots, thereby strengthening resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes coumestrol biosynthesis/accumulation; direct transcriptional targets not identified
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene intersection analysis; transient overexpression in soybean hairy roots; qRT-PCR; coumestrol metabolite quantification; P. sojae infection assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of metabolite accumulation; precise biochemical/regulatory mechanism unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; hairy root transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum inhibition of pathogen spore/cyst germination and suppression of P. sojae infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inducible by P. sojae; selected based on correlation between transcript abundance and coumestrol across tissues
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, PRJNA210431; metabolomics across 44 cultivars and tissue compendium
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro one-pot assays with purified dirigent protein did not produce a clear increase in coumestrol, so direct biochemical role remains unresolved; protein sequences were identical between Williams 82 and RPS1D.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In roots under P. sojae-responsive conditions; inferred from candidate screening and hairy root assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering disease-resistant soybean by increasing coumestrol accumulation, especially in susceptible cultivars.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: dirigent family protein; putative coumestrol biosynthesis regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased coumestrol accumulation and enhanced resistance to Phytophthora sojae in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dir4a emerged from multiomics candidate screening for coumestrol biosynthesis. 2. Transient overexpression in Williams 82 hairy roots significantly increased coumestrol accumulation. 3. Overexpression in susceptible P20 hairy roots elevated coumestrol and reduced P. sojae biomass.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Coumestrol biosynthesis module; direct targets unknown
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator/candidate determinant of coumestrol accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes coumestrol biosynthesis/accumulation; direct downstream genes not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene screening; transient overexpression in hairy roots; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; pathogen biomass quantification
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of coumestrol accumulation; exact mechanism unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; hairy root transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum inhibition of pathogen spore/cyst germination and suppression of infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: P. sojae-inducible and positively associated with coumestrol-rich tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Amino acid sequence identical between Williams 82 and RPS1D; in vitro one-pot biochemical assay was inconclusive.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to raise coumestrol and resistance in susceptible germplasm.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: dirigent family protein; putative coumestrol biosynthesis regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased coumestrol accumulation and enhanced resistance to Phytophthora sojae in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dir4b was identified by overlapping dirigent family homology, coumestrol–transcript correlation, and pathogen-inducible expression datasets. 2. Overexpression in Williams 82 hairy roots significantly increased coumestrol content. 3. Overexpression in susceptible P20 roots elevated coumestrol and reduced pathogen biomass.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Coumestrol biosynthesis module; direct molecular targets not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator/candidate determinant of coumestrol accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes coumestrol biosynthesis/accumulation; direct targets not identified
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multiomics candidate selection; hairy-root transient overexpression; qRT-PCR; coumestrol quantification; P. sojae resistance assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of metabolite accumulation; exact catalytic/regulatory role unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; hairy root transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum inhibition of pathogen spore/cyst germination and suppression of P. sojae infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by P. sojae and correlated with coumestrol accumulation across tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dir4a/4b were discussed together as functionally redundant positive factors; in vitro assay did not confirm a direct biochemical step.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering soybean resistance through coumestrol enhancement.
